skill.name: diagnosing-bugs
skill.description: A structured debugging workflow for hard bugs, failing behavior, and performance regressions.
skill.summary: Guide an AI coding assistant through reproducible bug diagnosis before fixing code.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against the two scanned files. The high weak-crypto alerts and medium command-execution alerts are false positives in Markdown prose, while the localhost URL appears in an interactive template with no outbound request code.

low_findings.title: Localhost URL in Interactive Template
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URL is a localhost example inside a human-in-the-loop prompt. The script prints prompts and reads answers; it does not make a network request or send data externally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is a prompt string for a user action. No curl, fetch, nc, or other network execution appears in the script.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection Is User Prompting
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged line asks the user whether an export action threw an error. It does not collect host information, enumerate the system, or run reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell function reads one user answer into a variable. There is no system command on this line and no use of the captured value for execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static command-execution hits are Markdown references to files, command names, debugging labels, or another skill. They are instructions for a debugging workflow, not executable code inside the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are in Markdown documentation and contain no shell script execution operators. The only executable file is the separate Bash template, which does not execute these strings.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Detector Matched Plain Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography alerts occur in ordinary prose, not cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or password handling logic was found in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are documentation text. They do not call MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic API.

content.user_title: Diagnose Bugs with a Reproducible Debug Loop
content.value_statement: Hard bugs waste time when teams chase guesses before a reliable signal exists. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through reproduction, minimization, hypotheses, instrumentation, and cleanup.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of a tight pass/fail loop before code changes.
Supports failing tests, HTTP scripts, CLI fixtures, browser automation, and human-in-the-loop scripts.
Requires reproduction and minimization before hypothesis testing.
Prompts ranked, falsifiable hypotheses before instrumentation.
Defines cleanup checks for debug logs, prototypes, and regression verification.
limitations: Does not diagnose bugs without access to a reproducible signal or useful artifact.
Does not provide project-specific test commands by itself.
May require user input for browser-only or environment-specific failures.
Does not replace domain knowledge for ranking likely causes.

use_cases.title: Stabilize a failing feature
use_cases.description: Create a reliable reproduction path before changing code.

use_cases.title: Investigate a performance regression
use_cases.description: Build a measurement baseline, then test ranked causes with focused probes.

use_cases.title: Prepare a debugging handoff
use_cases.description: Document the repro loop, minimized case, correct hypothesis, and cleanup status.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Bug Diagnosis
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this bug. First build a reproducible pass/fail loop, then summarize the exact command or user steps that prove it fails.

prompt_templates.title: Minimize a Reproduction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the diagnosing-bugs workflow to shrink this failing case. Keep removing inputs or steps until every remaining part is necessary.

prompt_templates.title: Rank Debugging Hypotheses
prompt_templates.prompt: After reproducing the issue, produce three to five ranked hypotheses. For each one, state the prediction that would prove or disprove it.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a Performance Regression
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this performance regression. Establish a baseline measurement, compare likely causes, instrument only the key boundaries, and verify the final fix.

output_examples.input: A checkout page fails only after adding two discount codes.
output_examples.output: The assistant first creates a repeatable reproduction, then minimizes the cart state and ranks likely causes before changing validation logic.

output_examples.input: A report endpoint became slower after the latest release.
output_examples.output: The assistant records a baseline, compares old and new behavior, checks query and cache hypotheses, and verifies the regression is gone.

output_examples.input: A browser-only export flow fails during manual testing.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses the human-in-the-loop template to collect the exact symptom, then turns the result into a tighter automated check if possible.

best_practices: Build the pass/fail loop before reading deeply or changing code.
Minimize the failing case before testing hypotheses.
Remove temporary instrumentation and rerun the original repro before finishing.
anti_patterns: Jumping to a single plausible cause before reproduction.
Adding broad logs that do not map to a specific prediction.
Declaring success after a narrow test passes while the original scenario remains unverified.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it for hard bugs, failing behavior, unclear errors, flaky issues, or performance regressions.

faq.question: Does it require a failing test?
faq.answer: No. It can use tests, HTTP scripts, CLI fixtures, browser automation, traces, or a human-in-the-loop script.

faq.question: Can it handle flaky bugs?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on increasing the reproduction rate until the issue can be debugged reliably.

faq.question: Will it fix the bug automatically?
faq.answer: It guides the diagnosis and verification process. Code changes still depend on the project and the confirmed cause.

faq.question: How does it help with performance regressions?
faq.answer: It asks for a baseline measurement first, then uses focused comparison and instrumentation before fixing.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the symptom, reproduction steps, logs, traces, screenshots, or access to the environment where the bug appears.
